author: Joanna Cismaru
description: This Instant Pot Mongolian Beef delivers takeout-style flavor at home in a fraction of the time. The beef is tender, saucy, and perfectly sweet-savory, making it a fast and satisfying dinner.
equipment: Instant Pot or electric pressure cooker
ingredients: 2 pounds flank steak, cut into thin strips
instructions: Toss flank steak strips with cornstarch in a large bowl and let sit for 5 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, soy sauce, water, garlic, ginger, brown sugar, and red pepper flakes. Set aside.
Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode.
Add half of the sesame oil to the pot. Once hot, add half of the beef and cook for 2-3 minutes until it begins to brown. Remove and repeat with remaining oil and beef.
Return all beef to the pot and cancel sauté mode.
Pour sauce over the beef and stir to combine.
Close the lid and set Instant Pot to Manual (High Pressure) for 10 minutes.
Quick release the pressure when cooking is complete.
Stir in green onions and garnish with sesame seeds. Serve over rice.
notes: Slicing Tip: Freeze steak for 20 minutes before slicing to make it easier to cut thin strips.
Meat Options: Top round steak, chicken breast, or chicken thighs can be substituted.
No Instant Pot: A stovetop version of Mongolian beef can be used instead.
title: Instant Pot Mongolian Beef
